q: What does 'ik begrijp het niet' mean?
e: This is a useful phrase combining 'ik' (I), 'begrijp' (understand), 'het' (it), and 'niet' (not), meaning 'I don't understand'.
Dromen means 'to dream'—the experience of vivid images and thoughts during sleep.
Genieten means 'to enjoy' or 'to take pleasure in' something.
Huilen means 'to cry' or 'to weep'—to shed tears, usually expressing sadness or strong emotion.
Ontdekken means 'to discover'—to find something previously unknown or unnoticed.
Ontwikkelen means 'to develop'—to grow, evolve, or increase in complexity or ability.
Vergelijken means 'to compare'—to examine similarities and differences between things.
Vergissen means 'to be mistaken' or 'to make a mistake'—to do or think something incorrectly.
Verkopen means 'to sell'—to exchange goods or services for money.
Vertrekken means 'to leave' or 'to depart'—to go away from a place.
Vertrouwen means 'to trust'—to have confidence in someone's reliability or honesty.
Verwachten means 'to expect'—to anticipate or assume that something will happen.
'In verwachting' is a phrase used to describe being pregnant—literally 'in expectation' of a baby's arrival.
Zwanger means 'pregnant'—describing a woman who is expecting a baby.
Zoeken means 'to search' or 'to look for'—to try to find something.
Zullen is a modal verb meaning 'will'—used to express future tense or intention in Dutch.
Bijten means 'to bite'—to cut or pierce with the teeth.
Noemen means 'to call' or 'to name'—to refer to someone or something by a particular name.
Het gereedschap means 'the tools'—implements used for manual work and repairs.
Het instrument can mean both 'the tool' in a general sense and 'the musical instrument' for playing music.
De schaar means 'the scissors'—a cutting tool with two blades that pivot together.
Knippen means 'to cut with scissors'—to use scissors to cut something.
De machine means 'the machine'—a mechanical or electrical device that performs a function.
De rekenmachine combines 'rekenen' (to calculate) and 'machine', meaning 'the calculator'.
De computer means 'the computer'—an electronic device for processing data.
Het slot means 'the lock'—a mechanism for fastening a door or container.
De sleutel means 'the key'—an object used to open a lock.
Openen means 'to open'—to move something so it's no longer closed.
De telefoon means 'the telephone'—a device for communicating over distances via sound.
Het potlood means 'the pencil'—a writing instrument with a graphite core.
De pen means 'the pen'—a writing instrument that uses ink.
Het papier means 'the paper'—a thin material made from plant fibers, used for writing.
De olie means 'the oil'—a slippery liquid used in cooking, machines, and more.
De benzine means 'the petrol' (British English) or 'the gasoline' (American English)—fuel for vehicles.
De brandstof means 'the fuel'—material burned to produce energy.
Het goud means 'the gold'—a precious yellow metal highly valued for jewelry and investment.
Het zilver means 'the silver'—a precious white metal used for jewelry and coins.
Het hout means 'the wood'—the hard material from tree trunks used for building and fuel.
De plank means 'the plank'—a flat piece of wood, often used for shelving or flooring.
De lucht means 'the air'—the invisible mixture of gases we breathe.
Het ijzer means 'the iron'—a common metal element used for tools and building.
Het staal means 'the steel'—an alloy of iron and carbon, stronger than pure iron.
De steen means 'the stone'—a hard mineral material found in nature.
Het stof (neuter) means 'the dust'—fine particles of matter suspended in air.
De stof (common) means 'the material' or 'the fabric'—cloth or textile material.
De aarde means 'the earth'—the planet we live on, or soil/ground.
Niets means 'nothing'—the absence of anything; not a single thing.
Alles means 'everything'—all things combined; the complete total.
'Een beetje' means 'a bit' or 'a little'—a small amount or degree of something.
